nodemon for automatic reload in headless dev (optional but minimal for iterative dev)
minimal backend API (only if required): express with a single route to serve static assets or simple media endpoints
basic headless environment vars: PORT, NODE_ENV=development
lightweight test harness (optional minimal): jest or built-in test runner with one smoke test
avoid heavy databases: use file-based storage or in-memory data for development instead of external DBs

steps.name: environment - persist NODE_ENV and PORT safely
steps.description: Create a /etc/profile.d script that persistently sets NODE_ENV=development and a default PORT while resolving npm global bin at shell startup (avoids embedding the current PATH at creation time). Ensures development env vars and PATH augmentation are available in future shells without brittle expansion.
steps.script_code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WORKSPACE="/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/flexible-video-preview-interface-14009-14011/backend"
# create profile script idempotently (replace if exists)
sudo tee /etc/profile.d/node_dev_env.sh >/dev/null <<'EOF'
# Node development environment
export NODE_ENV=development
export PORT="${PORT:-3000}"
# Resolve npm global bin at shell startup; do not expand current PATH now
export PATH="$(npm bin -g 2>/dev/null || echo /usr/local/bin):$PATH"
EOF
sudo chmod 644 /etc/profile.d/node_dev_env.sh
# quick validation: ensure node and npm exist
command -v node >/dev/null || { echo 'node missing' >&2; exit 2; }
command -v npm >/dev/null || { echo 'npm missing' >&2; exit 3; }

output_scripts.name: install
output_scripts.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WORKSPACE="/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/flexible-video-preview-interface-14009-14011/backend"
# idempotent creation: write literal content so runtime expansion (npm bin -g) happens on shell startup
sudo tee /etc/profile.d/node_dev_env.sh >/dev/null <<'EOF'
# Node development environment
export NODE_ENV=development
export PORT="${PORT:-3000}"
# Resolve npm global bin at shell startup; do not expand at creation time
export PATH="$(npm bin -g 2>/dev/null || echo /usr/local/bin):$PATH"
EOF
sudo chmod 644 /etc/profile.d/node_dev_env.sh
command -v node >/dev/null || { echo 'node missing' >&2; exit 2; }
command -v npm >/dev/null || { echo 'npm missing' >&2; exit 3; }

steps.summary: Created an idempotent /etc/profile.d script that persistently sets NODE_ENV=development, assigns a default PORT if not set, and resolves the npm global bin at each shell startup (without expanding the current PATH at creation time). Validated that node and npm are available on PATH. The script is written to .init/install.sh for autonomous execution.
steps.qa_summary: Core objective met: an idempotent /etc/profile.d script was created to set NODE_ENV=development, assign a default PORT when unset, and dynamically resolve npm global bin at shell startup. Engineer reports validating node and npm on PATH and saved the script to .init/install.sh for autonomous execution. No errors or questions were reported.

steps.name: testing - create jest smoke test (in-process) and run it
steps.description: Create a single Jest smoke test that uses supertest(app) to verify /media returns JSON {status:'ok'}. The test uses the exported app from index.js so no external process is spawned and the Jest runner is not terminated. Runs jest in-band for deterministic execution.

steps.summary: Created and executed an in-process Jest smoke test using supertest against the exported Express app. The script writes __tests__/smoke.test.js which requires ../index.js and asserts GET /media returns JSON {status:'ok'}. It then runs jest in-band via npx to produce deterministic results. The script is idempotent and uses the workspace path from the container context. Execution completed without needing architect intervention; if tests fail due to missing project scaffolding or dependencies (express/jest/supertest), the test run will surface those errors for the next fix step.

The engineer reported successful execution without escalation and provided clear evidence that the test file was written and run in-band via npx jest. No critical errors or security issues were reported.

steps.name: validation - start server, poll readiness, verify JSON, stop server
steps.description: Start the app with node (not nodemon) in background for deterministic lifecycle, poll the /media endpoint until success or timeout, validate the JSON response strictly via a small node-based parser, then stop the background process cleanly. Provides evidence the dev environment is operational.
steps.script_code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WORKSPACE="/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/flexible-video-preview-interface-14009-14011/backend"
cd "$WORKSPACE"
PORT=${PORT:-3000}
# start deterministic node process in background
node index.js &
PID=$!
# poll for readiness up to 15 seconds
SUCCESS=1
for i in {1..15}; do
  sleep 1
  RESPONSE=$(curl -sS --max-time 1 http://127.0.0.1:${PORT}/media || true)
  if [ -n "$RESPONSE" ]; then
    # validate JSON and expected key using node
    echo "$RESPONSE" | node -e "let s=''; process.stdin.on('data',c=>s+=c); process.stdin.on('end',()=>{try{const j=JSON.parse(s); if(j && j.status==='ok') process.exit(0); else process.exit(2);}catch(e){process.exit(3);}})" && { SUCCESS=0; break; }
  fi
done
# stop server
kill "$PID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
wait "$PID" 2>/dev/null || true
if [ $SUCCESS -eq 0 ]; then
  echo 'validation: OK'
  exit 0
else
  echo 'validation: FAILED' >&2
  exit 4
fi

output_scripts.name: build

output_scripts.name: start
output_scripts.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WORKSPACE="/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/flexible-video-preview-interface-14009-14011/backend"
cd "$WORKSPACE"
PORT=${PORT:-3000}
export PORT
# Start deterministic node process in background and record PID
node index.js &
PID=$!
echo "$PID" > .init/server.pid
# give very brief moment for startup
sleep 0.2
printf "%s\n" "$PID"

output_scripts.name: stop
output_scripts.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WORKSPACE="/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/flexible-video-preview-interface-14009-14011/backend"
cd "$WORKSPACE"
PID_FILE=.init/server.pid
if [ -f "$PID_FILE" ]; then
  PID=$(cat "$PID_FILE" || true)
  if [ -n "$PID" ]; then
    kill "$PID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    wait "$PID" 2>/dev/null || true
  fi
  rm -f "$PID_FILE"
fi

cd "$WORKSPACE"
# run jest in-band silently; exit non-zero on failure
npx jest --runInBand --silent

output_scripts.name: validation
output_scripts.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WORKSPACE="/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/flexible-video-preview-interface-14009-14011/backend"
cd "$WORKSPACE"
PORT=${PORT:-3000}
# Start deterministic node process in background (use start script to record PID)
bash .init/start.sh >/dev/null
PID_FILE=.init/server.pid
if [ ! -f "$PID_FILE" ]; then
  echo 'validation: FAILED - no pid file' >&2
  exit 4
fi
PID=$(cat "$PID_FILE")
SUCCESS=1
# poll for readiness up to 15 seconds
for i in {1..15}; do
  sleep 1
  RESPONSE=$(curl -sS --max-time 1 "http://127.0.0.1:${PORT}/media" || true)
  if [ -n "$RESPONSE" ]; then
    # validate JSON and expected key using node
    echo "$RESPONSE" | node -e "let s=''; process.stdin.on('data',c=>s+=c); process.stdin.on('end',()=>{try{const j=JSON.parse(s); if(j && j.status==='ok') process.exit(0); else process.exit(2);}catch(e){process.exit(3);}})" && { SUCCESS=0; break; }
  fi
done
# stop server cleanly
bash .init/stop.sh >/dev/null || true
if [ "$SUCCESS" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo 'validation: OK'
  exit 0
else
  echo 'validation: FAILED' >&2
  exit 4
fi

steps.summary: Executed validation step artifacts: created lightweight command scripts to build (install deps), start the Node app deterministically, stop it, run tests, and perform the requested validation which starts node in background, polls /media until success or timeout, validates JSON response via a small node parser, then stops the process. Scripts operate in the workspace /home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/flexible-video-preview-interface-14009-14011/backend, are non-interactive, use strict error handling, and produce clear exit codes. No architect help required.
steps.qa_summary: Engineer reports successful execution of the validation step: created and ran non-interactive scripts that start Node in background, polled /media until ready, validated JSON via a Node parser, and stopped the server cleanly. Summary indicates strict error handling, clear exit codes, and no errors or need for escalation.

reasoning: Address analyst feedback by hardening lifecycle management, avoiding unsafe expansions when persisting environment variables, and making tests and validation deterministic and in-process. Persist NODE_ENV and PORT via /etc/profile.d while deferring npm global-bin resolution to shell startup to avoid embedding the current PATH. Modify scaffolding so the Express app is exported and a start() function is exposed; when run directly the module starts the server, but when required by tests it only provides the app object for in-process testing. Pin dev and prod dependency versions for repeatability and install supertest in the dependencies step so tests don't inline-install. Tests use supertest(app) and call server.close() indirectly by not starting a separate server; validation starts the app via node in the background, polls for readiness with a timeout loop (no fixed sleeps), asserts JSON status strictly via a small node-based JSON check, and stops the background PID cleanly. Scripts follow the required shebang and strict error handling, use the authoritative workspace path, avoid expanding PATH at file-creation time, and keep concise, idempotent, non-interactive commands.

change_logs.message: Refined initial plan to fix critical lifecycle and environment issues flagged by analyst. Modified env-001 to avoid expanding $PATH at write time and compute npm global bin at shell startup. Updated scaffold-002 to export the Express app and provide a start() function so tests can run in-process. Adjusted test-004 to use supertest(app) and not kill the Node process; removed process.kill and rely on in-process testing. Moved supertest install to deps-003 and pinned dependency versions (express, nodemon, jest, supertest) for repeatability. Rewrote validation-005 to start node index.js (not nodemon), poll for readiness with timeout, verify JSON response using a small node check, and stop the server PID cleanly. These changes directly address the CRITICAL and HIGH feedback items for steps env-001, scaffold-002, test-004, deps-003, and validation-005.

qa_summary: The plan covers all required categories (environment, scaffolding, dependencies, testing, validation) and provides coherent, idempotent, non-interactive scripts that use the authoritative workspace path. Core runtime and tools are present in the image; the plan avoids unnecessary global installs, persists NODE_ENV/PORT via /etc/profile.d, scaffolds an Express app exported for in-process tests, installs pinned local deps, creates a Jest+supertest smoke test, and implements a deterministic validation step that starts, polls, verifies and stops the server. No CRITICAL-level gaps were found. A few robustness and best-practice issues (see items) should be fixed before running autonomously in a CI-like environment.
qa_issues: Step 'test-004' swallows test failures by appending '|| true' to the jest run, causing the test step to always succeed even when tests fail.
Step 'deps-003' performs two separate npm install invocations; these can be combined for efficiency and to ensure lockfile consistency.

Step 'env-001' writes a profile that contains command-substitution ("$(npm bin -g ...)") which will run at shell startup — this is intentional, but if npm is missing or not in PATH at login time it could cause noisy errors. The script validates npm/node after creating the profile, but consider ensuring profile is robust when sourced in environments without npm present.

Step scripts use the workspace path assigned to WORKSPACE as a hardcoded string. This meets the requirement to use the container info workspace, but the plan should document that WORKSPACE must match the authoritative path (it already does).

Step 'deps-003' pins using caret ranges (e.g., express@^4.18.2, jest@^29.0.0). Caret ranges can lead to different installed versions over time; if strict reproducibility is desired, use exact versions or commit a lockfile.

Step 'validation-005' starts the app via 'node index.js &' and relies on killing PID; if index.js spawns a child process or daemonizes, the kill/wait may not stop all processes. Document that index.js must not fork/daemonize (current scaffold is OK), or add a more robust stop (pgrep/killall or store server PID in a file).

qa_recommendations.description: Modify Step 'test-004' to not mask test failures. Remove the trailing '|| true' so the script fails on test failures (npx --yes jest --runInBand --silent). This ensures failing tests surface as failures in automated runs.

Combine npm installs in Step 'deps-003' into a single idempotent command to reduce network calls and ensure consistent lockfile generation (e.g., npm i --no-audit --silent express@4.18.2 --save && npm i --no-audit --silent --save-dev nodemon@2.0.22 jest@29.0.0 supertest@6.3.3 or use a single 'npm i' invocation that includes both prod and dev deps). Also ensure package-lock.json is generated/committed if reproducibility is required.

Harden Step 'env-001' profile script to avoid emitting an error when npm is not present at login. For example, wrap the npm bin expansion in a subshell that redirects errors to /dev/null or use a fallback PATH without relying on npm at shell startup: export NPM_GLOB_BIN="$(command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1 && npm bin -g || echo /usr/local/bin)"; export PATH="$NPM_GLOB_BIN:$PATH". This avoids noisy errors when the profile is sourced in minimal shells.

In Step 'deps-003' prefer exact pinned versions (no caret) or include a package-lock.json to guarantee exact installs across runs. If caret ranges are acceptable, document that dependency drift is expected and acceptable for development images.

In Step 'validation-005' consider writing the server PID to a temporary file and/or using a more robust stop procedure (e.g., use the exported start() to programmatically stop the server if started in-process, or use pgrep/pgrep -f to find children). Currently the scaffolded index.js returns the server object when start() is used; consider using that in validation to call server.close() for a cleaner shutdown, or ensure the background process doesn't spawn children.

Remove or justify 'npx --yes' in Step 'test-004'. Since jest is installed as a devDependency in deps-003, npx is not strictly required; running 'npm test' or './node_modules/.bin/jest' is clearer and avoids implicit network installs in environments lacking npm cache.

Optional: Add a lightweight .gitignore and package-lock.json generation step in scaffolding to avoid accidentally committing node_modules. Also consider adding a minimal README or developer notes file describing how to run dev vs. production, environment variables, and the expected behavior of the validation step.
